idea.hook: Want to visit our colossal neighboring galaxy, Andromeda?
facts: Andromeda is approximately 2.537 million light-years away from the Milky Way.
Even traveling at the speed of light (300,000 km/s), it would take you 2.537 million years to reach it.
Our fastest spacecraft, the Parker Solar Probe, would take nearly 4 billion years to cover that distance!
idea.payoff: Good news: you don't have to! Andromeda is already hurtling towards us for a spectacular galactic collision in just 4.5 billion years!
idea.title: Our Galactic Road Trip: Andromeda! 🚀🌌

scenes.text: Want to visit our colossal neighbor, Andromeda?
scenes.visual: Stunning CGI shot of the Milky Way galaxy with the faint spiral of the Andromeda galaxy visible in the distance, bathed in stars.
Stylized icons of the Milky Way and Andromeda galaxies with a massive, glowing distance line spanning between them. Starfield background.

scenes.text: Even at light speed (300,000 km/s), it'd take 2.537 MILLION years!
scenes.visual: A vibrant light beam traveling rapidly across the screen from a Milky Way icon on the left to an Andromeda icon on the right, with a clock icon ticking rapidly.

scenes.text: Our fastest probe, Parker Solar, would take ~4 BILLION years!
scenes.visual: A tiny, barely perceptible spacecraft icon (Parker Solar Probe) moving incredibly slowly from a Milky Way icon towards an Andromeda icon, emphasizing the immense scale and time.

scenes.text: Good news: You don't have to! Andromeda is coming to US in ~4.5 Billion Years!
scenes.visual: Dramatic animation showing the Andromeda galaxy rapidly growing larger and closer, hurtling towards the Milky Way. Ends with a beautiful, stylized galactic collision/merger.
